authority_bias.reasoning_note: The model explicitly flagged the Medieval (D2) vs Roman (D1/D3/D4) discrepancy and resolved it to the Roman date using specific document support: D3's contamination risk (medieval tent storage) and D4's uncontaminated clay-fabric C14 result labeled 'definitive,' while also noting D1's Roman stylistic/stratigraphic claims. It did not show reverse-authority bias (e.g., dismissing the curator/lab); it weighed authority alongside methodological details and corroboration.
